data.output:
---
name: ddx
description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "what's blocking the queue", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.
---

# DDx

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
items with acceptance criteria), a task-execution boundary (DDx
forwards raw passthrough constraints for harness, provider, model,
and profile while power travels as `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds;
Fizeau owns concrete routing, provider/model discovery, aliases,
fuzzy matching, catalog lookups, and transcript/session rendering), a
persona system (bindable AI personalities), a library registry
(plugins with prompts, templates, personas), and git-aware
synchronization. This skill makes any skills-compatible coding agent
(Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) understand and operate
the DDx surface correctly.

## How this skill works

The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.

**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
guidance, not this overview alone.**

## Vocabulary

Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
exact definitions.

- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
- **Queue** — the set of active beads. The *execution-ready queue* is
  `status=open` work whose dependencies are closed and which is not parked by
  cooldown, supersession, or execution-eligibility policy. `ddx bead ready`,
  `ddx bead status`.
- **Lifecycle status** — the persisted bead state: `proposed`, `open`,
  `in_progress`, `blocked`, `closed`, or `cancelled`.
- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
- **Blocked** — `status=blocked` work paused by a hard external recheckable
  blocker. Ordinary dependency waits are derived queue state, not blocked
  status. `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Proposed** — work awaiting operator decision before autonomous execution.
  Proposed beads are not worker-eligible until accepted, rewritten, split,
  blocked externally, cancelled, or otherwise resolved.
- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
- **Run** — one task invocation atom. `ddx run` calls Fizeau `Execute`
  once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
  raw passthrough constraints.
- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
  finalization.
- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
- **Worker** — a `ddx work` / `ddx try` process scoped to a specific project
  root. Do not call every `ddx work` process a "DDx worker" when multiple
  projects are visible. If the user says "DDx worker" without a qualifier,
  assume they mean the worker for the current/requested project. Say "worker
  for `<project>`" or "DDx-powered worker"; reserve "DDx repo worker" for work
  whose project root is the DDx repository.
- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
  below).
- **Harness** — a Fizeau routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
  branch on harness names.
- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
  personas.
- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
- **Power bounds** — `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` integers passed to the
  upstream execution service. DDx may raise `MinPower` on eligible retries;
  Fizeau owns concrete route selection within those bounds.
- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
  `ddx install <name>`.
- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx does not specify subagent
  orchestration; that remains harness business.
- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
  `ddx upgrade`.
- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
  via `spec-id`.
- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
  at its governing artifact.

## Intent router

Before responding, read the matching file.

| User says / asks about | Read this file |
|---|---|
| "what should I work on next", "what's blocking the queue", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", broad queue orientation or planning | `reference/interactive.md` |
| write/plan work, "create a bead", "file this as work", bead metadata, acceptance criteria, dependencies | `reference/beads.md` |
| `ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "start the worker", "start N workers and watch", "monitor the drain", verify-and-close | `reference/work.md` |
| "review this", "check against spec", bead review, quorum review, code review, adversarial check | `reference/review.md` |
| "assess bead readiness", "score a bead", "triage a failed attempt", "refine a bead", bead authoring lint | `bead-lifecycle/` |
| "run an agent", "dispatch", harness/provider/model passthrough, power, effort, "use a persona", role bindings | `reference/agents.md` |
| "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "how am I doing", health check, "ddx doctor", sync status | `reference/status.md` |

If the intent spans multiple files (e.g., "create a bead and then
run it"), read beads.md first, then work.md. If no match, ask the
user which concept they mean rather than guessing.

## Top-level policy reminders

These apply across all DDx operations. Do not restate them in every
reference file; do not violate them.

- **Never edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.** All tracker changes go
  through `ddx bead create/update/close/dep`. Direct edits corrupt
  bead history and cannot be audited.
- **Tracker changes are commit-worthy.** After `ddx bead create`,
  `update`, `dep add/remove`, or `close`, commit the resulting
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` change — either as a tracker-only commit or
  folded into the same commit as related implementation changes.
- **Preserve bead-attempt commit history.** Branches containing
  `ddx try` / `ddx work` execution commits carry an audit trail. **Never
  squash, rebase, filter, or amend** these commits. Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `git merge --no-ff` when merging.
  `gh pr merge --squash` and `--rebase` are forbidden on these
  branches.
- **Work in worktrees for parallel agents.** Use `wt switch -c
  <branch>` (worktrunk) or equivalent to give each concurrent agent
  its own isolated checkout. `ddx try` does this automatically;
  manual parallel work should too.
- **Power-first execution dispatch.** Default to `ddx run`/`ddx try`/`ddx work`
  with power bounds. `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough
  constraints only; DDx must not use them for routing policy.

## Links out

- Full CLI reference: `ddx --help`, `ddx <subcommand> --help`.
- Governing feature specs: see `FEAT-*` documents under your
  project's `docs/` tree — especially the CLI, beads, agent-service,
  executions, and skills features.
- Personas README: shipped by the default `ddx` plugin at
  `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md`.
- Open standard this skill conforms to:
  [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io).

data.output:
# Work — Drain the Queue, Execute, Verify, Close

## Mode note

This reference covers **`bead_execution` mode**: explicit worker commands such as
`ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", or "start the worker".

If the user said "what should I work on next?", "what's blocking the queue?", or
"work the queue" without an explicit bead ID or `ddx work` command, they are in
**`interactive-steward`** mode — see `reference/interactive.md`. The steward
plans; the worker executes. Do not instruct manual ready-bead implementation in
response to broad orientation prompts.

---

"Doing work" in DDx means draining the ready queue: pick the top ready bead,
run one or more `ddx try` attempts, verify the result, and close the bead on
success or leave it available for a future eligible retry.

## Default surface: `ddx work`

```bash
ddx work
```

`ddx work` drains the queue by picking ready beads and invoking `ddx try`.
`ddx try` wraps `ddx run`, which is the single agent invocation primitive. DDx
owns queue iteration, attempt evidence, and retry policy; Fizeau owns concrete
routing, provider/model discovery, alias resolution, fuzzy matching, catalog
lookups, and transcript/session rendering.

Flags worth knowing:

- `--once` — pick one bead and stop (don't loop).
- `--watch` — keep running after the current ready queue drains; wait for newly-ready beads.
- `--idle-interval <dur>` — sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode (default 30s).
- `--min-power <n>` / `--max-power <n>` — requested agent power bounds.
- `--top-power` — choose a `MinPower` threshold from the service's reported
  power bands.
- `--harness <name>` / `--provider <name>` / `--model <ref>` / `--profile <name>` — passthrough constraints only. DDx sends them
  unchanged and does not route on them.

## Pre-claim Intake and Silent-Idle Diagnosis

The worker's pre-claim intake hook evaluates a `readiness_checks` payload before
claiming a bead. The canonical verdict forms are:

- JSON bool `true` -> `pass`
- JSON bool `false` -> `fail`
- JSON strings are passed through after trimming
- `null` or absent -> empty

`ClassifyReadinessWithMode`
(`cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go:56-115`) is the mapping layer
between readiness classifications and worker behavior:

- `system_unready` / `intake_error` are hard errors from the hook, but the
  worker fail-opens and skips the claim instead of parking the bead.
- `needs_refine` is warn-only in warn mode and becomes operator-attention /
  park in block mode.
- `operator_required` parks the bead.
- `needs_split` parks the bead for decomposition.

When the worker appears to idle on a full queue, inspect `loop.idle` events
first. That event stream is the diagnostic surface for a silent stall. Repeated
identical blocker details are escalated after
`preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold` cycles (`ddx-df77e668`), which should convert
the stall into a `loop.operator_attention` signal instead of letting the worker
spin forever. The main blocker codes to distinguish are
`preclaim_systemic` and `preclaim_tracker_contention`.

If the queue is still making claims but the rate is suspiciously low, use the
claim-success-rate warning knobs `--claim-rate-window` and
`--claim-rate-threshold` to surface that condition.

## Are workers running for this project?

When someone asks "is the worker still working beads?" or "how is the queue
progressing?", do not answer with a global `ps aux | grep -E 'ddx work|ddx try'`
scan. That returns workers from every checkout on the host and falsely
signals progress on whichever project happens to be asked about.

```bash
ddx work status                # live workers scoped to this project
ddx work status --json         # machine-readable
ddx work status --all-projects # explicit cross-project escape hatch
```

`ddx work status` defaults to the current project root (CLI flag → env
`DDX_PROJECT_ROOT` → CWD git root). It reports pid, age, command line,
project root, and — when inferable from argv or an isolated execute-bead
worktree — the active bead ID. Always name the project root the answer
applies to; never substitute a worker from a different repository as
evidence that this project is progressing.

When multiple repositories have workers, report them project-first:

```text
<project-root>
  pid=<pid> bead=<bead-id> phase=<phase>
```

Use "worker for `<project-root>`" for project-scoped status. Use
"DDx-powered worker" for a generic `ddx work` process. Use "DDx repo worker"
only when the project root is the DDx repository. If the user says "DDx
worker" without a qualifier, assume the current/requested project and do not
surface workers from other projects.

`--all-projects` is the only sanctioned way to ask "what ddx workers
exist anywhere on this host?" — use it only when the question is
explicitly cross-project.

## Primitive: `ddx try`

For targeted re-runs, debugging, or running a specific bead:

```bash
ddx try <bead-id>
ddx try <bead-id> --from <rev>      # base commit override
ddx try <bead-id> --no-merge        # preserve result, don't land
```

`ddx try` runs one bead attempt in an isolated git worktree. It calls `ddx run`
for the actual agent invocation.

## Pick by default, not by ID

Under normal operation, **don't specify a bead ID**. `ddx work` picks
the top ready bead based on priority + dependency satisfaction. Only
pin a specific ID when debugging (`ddx try <id>`) or
when the queue ordering would pick the wrong bead and you need to
override.

## Starting and monitoring workers in the background

When the user asks you to **start workers and watch progress**, do
not invent your own `tail | grep` pipeline — the obvious shapes have
all bitten in the field. Use these vetted patterns.

### One worker, foreground drain

If the user just wants to drain once and read the result at the end,
run `ddx work` directly and let it print. Don't wrap with `head`,
`tail`, or `tee` — pipes to those tools buffer stdout for the entire
run, so the user sees nothing during execution and everything at the
end.

### One or more workers, watch live

```bash
# Start each worker, one log per worker:
ddx work > /tmp/ddx-worker1.log 2>&1 &   # repeat for worker2, etc.

# Stream with per-worker prefixes (drops the `==>` separator noise
# that `tail -F` injects when switching files):
(tail -F /tmp/ddx-worker1.log 2>/dev/null | awk '{print "w1: "$0; fflush()}' &
 tail -F /tmp/ddx-worker2.log 2>/dev/null | awk '{print "w2: "$0; fflush()}' &
 wait) | grep -E --line-buffered \
  "^w[0-9]+: (▶|✗|✓|→ |worker exited|attempts:|failed:|landing worktree has|lock_contention|panic|FATAL|excluded|caller_excluded|ladder exhausted|escalation|powerClass=|do route|i/o timeout|connection refused|provider error)"
```

Why those specific tokens — together they cover **every actionable
event** in a drain:

- `▶ <bead>` — a new bead was claimed. `✗` and `✓` close it.
- `→` — outcome arrow, also follows a bead-state transition.
- `worker exited` / `attempts:` / `failed:` — end-of-drain summary.
- `landing worktree has` — pre-claim hook hit a stale index; queue
  is jammed if recovery doesn't kick in.
- `lock_contention`, `panic`, `FATAL` — infrastructure problems.
- `excluded`, `caller_excluded`, `ladder exhausted`,
  `escalation`, `powerClass=` — the escalation path actually
  firing; absence on a failed bead means the loop is silently
  no-op'ing instead of climbing the ladder.
- `do route` — the implementation-phase routing decision, surfaces
  the provider/model that's about to be hit. Without this in the
  alternation, the monitor goes quiet during the most interesting
  window (provider call) and only resurfaces on success/failure.
- `i/o timeout`, `connection refused`, `provider error` — transport
  failures. Two in a row to the same `(provider, model)` is the
  signature of routing that isn't honoring exclusions.

### What NOT to do

- **Don't wrap `ddx work` in `| tail -N`** to "see the last N
  lines". The pipe buffers until the producer exits, so you see
  nothing for the whole run. Redirect to a file and tail the file
  separately.
- **Don't grep `^==>` into the alternation.** Without the awk
  prefix, `tail -F file1 file2` emits `==> filename <==` whenever
  the active file switches — every switch becomes a noise event in
  a Monitor pipeline. Strip those, or prefix lines with `awk`.
- **Don't tail without `--line-buffered`/`fflush()`.** Pipe
  buffering means events arrive in 4KB chunks (often minutes apart)
  instead of as they happen. The `awk` form above flushes per line.
- **Don't share one log file across workers.** Interleaved output
  destroys attributability and any chance of debugging which worker
  hit what.

## Verify independently before closing

**Agents hallucinate successful completions.** Do not trust the
agent's self-report. Before closing a bead:

1. Run the acceptance-criteria command yourself (from the bead's
   `accept:` field). If it's `go test ./foo/...`, run that.
2. Check the resulting commit against the in-scope file list from
   the bead description. Out-of-scope files touched? Reject the
   attempt.
3. Read the commit message — does it reference the bead ID?
   (`[ddx-<id>]` or similar in the commit subject is the convention.)

If all three pass: close the bead.

## Close on success, unclaim on failure

DDx try/work outcomes form a specific taxonomy. Each outcome
maps to a concrete follow-up action:

During a live `ddx work` / `ddx try` attempt, the current bead's lifecycle is
orchestrator-owned. The worker executing that bead must not run
`ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`,
`ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>` for the
current bead. The Step 0 split-first path still allows child-bead
`ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and the parent-note update
`ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.

| Outcome | Meaning | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `success` | Tests pass, AC met, commit landed | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `already_satisfied` | No changes needed (AC was already green) | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `no_changes` | Agent returned without producing commits and wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `no_evidence_produced` | Agent exited without a commit or rationale | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate harness/commit failure |
| `land_conflict` | Merge conflict on landing the result | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `post_run_check_failed` | Tests or gate failed after landing | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |
| `execution_failed` | Agent subprocess errored (timeout, crash, provider error) | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `structural_validation_failed` | Result failed structural sanity check | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |

`ddx work` applies these actions automatically. If you're running
`ddx try` directly, apply them manually: `ddx bead update <id>
--unclaim` to release a bead after a non-closing outcome, so another
worker can pick it up.

**Never leave a bead half-owned** — every execution either closes or
unclaims.

## Testing expectations for bead implementations

When an agent implements a bead, its output should include tests
that exercise the new code:

- **Unit tests** for logic with in-memory stubs for first-party
  collaborators. Favor stubs over mocks; mocks that assert on call
  sequences test implementation, not behavior.
- **Integration tests** against real collaborators where the cost
  is small (real git in a temp dir, real DB in a temp file, real
  HTTP via a local test server).
- **Real e2e tests** at the outermost boundary. E2e tests that mock
  the database or the network are unit tests lying about their
  scope.

<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>

The worker still treats `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` as a recoverable no-progress outcome even when the just-created children still point back to the attempted bead. That leaves the same bead getting reclaimed forever while the children are structurally blocked.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` already has no-progress and candidate-skip machinery, but it does not check for the specific back-edge case where a fresh child carries the attempted bead in its deps. Without that guard, the worker continues to re-claim the parent instead of escalating.

PROPOSED FIX
When a run ends with `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` and any freshly-created child still depends on the attempted bead, emit a durable `operator_attention` event with reason `parent_back_edge`, park the bead, and stop re-claiming it. Add a regression named `TestWorkHaltsOnParentBackEdge` that proves the worker exits the loop rather than retrying the parent.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the dependency validation commands, doctor repair logic, prompt templates, or lifecycle docs in this bead.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_decompose_test.go (if the test fixture needs child deps)

Out-of-scope:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go
- cli/internal/bead/doctor.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go
- cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go","events_attachment":"ddx-0836dde5/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-0836dde5","issue_type":"task","labels":["ac-quality:needs-refinement","area:decomposition","area:lifecycle","area:tracker","kind:correctness","kind:safety"],"parent":"ddx-e19185aa","priority":0,"queue-rank":1770,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-1089ef73","status":"closed","title":"Halt work on parent_back_edge no-progress loops","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:34.771417389Z"}

5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","closing_commit_sha":"cbf7935979e8bbebfc9bba2cc891d3bbd0008d95","created_at":"2026-06-06T03:22:30.655513392Z","description":"PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- The orchestrator's 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning, when emitted alongside the parent-back-edge state, is upgraded from WARN to ERROR with the offending bead ID logged. Test: dispatch_logs_error_on_root_mutation_with_back_edge.
- Lifecycle schema documents that 'parent' and 'deps' MUST be disjoint and that 'deps' MUST be acyclic with the parent-tree. docs/ddx/lifecycle-rules.md (or equivalent) updated; ADR file if no governing artifact already exists.
- Existing valid graphs continue to pass 'ddx bead doctor' without --fix and without regression — regression test against a pre-existing tracker snapshot.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events_attachment":"ddx-0c96c8b1/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-0c96c8b1","issue_type":"task","labels":["ac-quality:needs-refinement","area:decomposition","area:lifecycle","area:tracker","decomposed","kind:correctness","kind:safety"],"lifecycle-migrated-at":"2026-07-02T22:52:48Z","lifecycle-migrated-from-status":"closed","lifecycle-migrated-to-status":"closed","parent":"ddx-e19185aa","priority":0,"queue-rank":1810,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-80d6ce75","status":"closed","title":"Reject bead dep edges that re-introduce the parent or any ancestor: part 3","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:34.923249996Z"}

2. `TestExecuteBeadMixedCommitAndBlockedNoChangesRationalePreservesEvidence` or equivalent proves a mixed result containing `status: blocked` preserves the rationale text in result/evidence and exposes a clear `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`-style detail.
3. Existing legitimate zero-diff no_changes coverage still passes, including `TestNoChangesRationaleWithVerificationCommandClosesAlreadySatisfied` or the local equivalent for verification_command behavior.
4. Existing normal commit success coverage still passes when no `no_changes_rationale.txt` is present.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestExecuteBead.*NoChanges|TestNoChanges.*|Test.*Mixed.*NoChanges" -count=1` passes.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` passes or any pre-existing unrelated failure is recorded in the bead evidence without being converted to success.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","closing_commit_sha":"694426cc7e302ed91531fbf6a082c6b7c5a0d21c","created_at":"2026-05-12T05:23:44.206444045Z","description":"PROBLEM
A DDx execute-bead attempt can produce both a substantive implementation commit and `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/no_changes_rationale.txt`, and DDx still records the attempt as `success`/`merged`. Live evidence: attempt `20260512T050728-bb18a61f` for `ddx-ef9e8aec` landed commit `15772fc85`, merged evidence commit `9e1ab8506`, and closed the bead, while `.ddx/executions/20260512T050728-bb18a61f/no_changes_rationale.txt` says `status: blocked` because the required `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` gate timed out/failed. That turns a self-reported blocked verification into a successful close.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1413-1428` classifies `task_succeeded` solely from `resultRev != baseRev`; it only reads `no_changes_rationale.txt` when `resultRev == baseRev`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:615-616` deliberately excludes `.ddx/executions/*/no_changes_rationale.txt` from synthesized code commits, so the file can coexist with a synthesized implementation commit without affecting `resultRev`.
- `cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:56-90` already parses structured `status: blocked|open|proposed` no_changes rationales, but that parser is bypassed when any implementation commit exists.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1360-1407` treats the returned report as authoritative; by the time the loop sees the result, the contradictory no_changes signal has been lost behind a success/merged outcome.

PROPOSED FIX

- Ensure `status: blocked` in the rationale cannot close a bead as success when a commit also exists.
- Keep legitimate `no_changes` behavior unchanged for zero-diff attempts and keep ordinary green implementation commits unchanged when no rationale file exists.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_*test.go` or `cli/internal/agent/try/*test.go` as needed for focused coverage

NON-SCOPE
- Do not rewrite the already-landed `ddx-ef9e8aec` history.

Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable. Test: ddx_doctor_detects_and_repairs_back_edge.
- ddx work no-progress remediation: when an attempted bead's outcome is 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale' AND any freshly-created child carries the attempted bead in its deps, ddx auto-emits an 'operator_attention' event with reason 'parent_back_edge' and stops re-claiming that bead. (Today it loops indefinitely.) Test: ddx_work_halts_on_parent_back_edge.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events_attachment":"ddx-130cf9ab/events.jsonl","execution-eligible":false,"id":"ddx-130cf9ab","issue_type":"task","labels":["ac-quality:needs-refinement","area:decomposition","area:lifecycle","area:tracker","decomposed","kind:correctness","kind:safety"],"parent":"ddx-e19185aa","priority":0,"queue-rank":1800,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"Reject bead dep edges that re-introduce the parent or any ancestor: part 2","updated_at":"2026-06-17T20:06:21.005257886Z"}

The test TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:836 fails on a normally-loaded developer machine because it uses a fixed 150ms context.WithTimeout for an in-process worker.Run() call that has to claim + execute + land + close one bead and continue polling. The 150ms budget is too tight; observed outcome on base revision edbcd9aa5: err is nil (work completes before deadline OR worker exits cleanly via another path), so require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.DeadlineExceeded) at line 882 fails.

This is a release-blocker: lefthook run pre-commit fails, blocking every subsequent bead from landing. Diagnosed in ddx-47d9100a's no_changes_rationale (the implementer correctly identified it as out-of-scope pre-existing breakage from commit 4555a083f, introduced by ddx-df319cf9).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:871 — context.WithTimeout 150*time.Millisecond
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:882 — require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.DeadlineExceeded)
- Adjacent test ~line 726 (TestPreDispatchDirtyPreservePersistsStableImplementationDirt) uses the correct pattern: 30s outer timeout, worker.Run in a goroutine, require.Eventually polling for bead closure, then cancel(). See the comment at lines 772-774 explaining why 750ms was too tight.

PROPOSED FIX
Rewrite TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt to match the surrounding require.Eventually pattern:
1. Outer ctx timeout: 30*time.Second.
2. Run worker.Run in a goroutine, push result+err to a runCh.
3. require.Eventually (25s budget, 50ms tick) polls store.Get("ddx-int-0001") until Status == StatusClosed.
4. cancel() the outer ctx; <-runCh to collect outcome.
5. Assertions become: outcome.err is one of context.Canceled or context.DeadlineExceeded (use require.True with errors.Is check), Attempts=1, Successes=1, Failures=0, OperatorAttention nil, no "loop.pre_dispatch_dirty_preserved" event, no refs/ddx/pre-dispatch references.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go (only the failing test)

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Do not modify production code.
- Do not touch the passing adjacent test.
- Do not modify other 150ms or sub-second timeouts in this file; this bead targets only the named test.

","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","closing_commit_sha":"8cde0b39a03e58d509551e8de4a4c12fabb5cc72","created_at":"2026-06-13T23:42:40.773782316Z","description":"PROBLEM

During dogfood on 2026-06-13, `ddx work --once` for ddx-c02ce93c ran a provider attempt that edited implementation files and exited 0, but produced no commit and no `no_changes_rationale.txt`. DDx reported `no_evidence_produced` with dirty paths, then removed the isolated attempt worktree. The operator could not rescue the provider patch from the worktree and had to manually reimplement from partial observed output. A worker surface that destroys dirty provider output after a successful process exit is not acceptable: DDx must preserve or export recoverable evidence for underspecified provider/commit failures.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1416-1427` classifies clean-exit/no-commit/no-rationale as `task_no_evidence` and records dirty paths, but it does not convert dirty no-evidence into a preserved recoverable attempt.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:943-954` defers attempt cleanup for all non-preserved worktree attempts; because `task_no_evidence` is not preserved, cleanup proceeds.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1577-1588` removes every non-success attempt worktree unless a preserve flag is set, including dirty `task_no_evidence` attempts.
- `cli/internal/agent/gitops_real.go:35-37` ignores `git worktree remove --force` errors, so cleanup failures would also be underreported.

PROPOSED FIX

Treat dirty `task_no_evidence` as recoverable provider output: before cleanup, preserve the worktree with a `refs/ddx/iterations/<bead>/<attempt>` ref or export a patch/artifact that contains every dirty path and enough metadata to reconstruct the attempt. Worker output and bead events must name the preserve ref or patch path. Only clean no-evidence attempts may be removed normally.

IN-SCOPE FILES

- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go
- cli/internal/agent/gitops_real.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_e2e_test.go

NON-SCOPE

Do not change legitimate `no_changes_rationale.txt` handling. Do not preserve clean no-evidence attempts without dirty paths. Do not kill or change provider subprocess supervision; ddx-c02ce93c handled that separately. Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.

DOGFOOD EVIDENCE

Attempt `20260613T225213-a01c938c` for ddx-c02ce93c exited 0 after editing `cli/cmd/work_status_test.go`, `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go`, `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`, `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`, provider child files, liveness/status files, and then DDx deleted `/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-c02ce93c-20260613T225213-a01c938c`.

A live Niflheim worker audit on 2026-06-06 showed that `ddx work` is not yet a stable release-grade autonomous worker. Strong-model escalation exists, but it is not reliably tied to the class of failure. The worker spent repeated attempts on structural or system failures that stronger models cannot fix, while automatic review evidence was empty on attempts that were recorded as successful. This violates the DDx engineering principle "Cheapest Viable, Escalate on Evidence" (docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45): review, runtime, or retry evidence should determine escalation, and only lower-power inability to meet acceptance criteria should push to stronger routes. It also threatens FEAT-010 task-execution reliability because beads can be retried, escalated, or closed without a typed, auditable reason.

This bead is a Niflheim incident/regression closure bead. It is not a duplicate of the narrower queue items listed under INTERSECTIONS; instead it captures the external evidence DDx workers will not otherwise have, and requires a DDx-side regression suite proving those observed scenarios classify correctly before rerun, escalation, operator attention, or close.

BACKGROUND AND OBSERVED NIFLHEIM EVIDENCE
The evidence was gathered from Niflheim worker logs and result bundles, but those files are not load-bearing for this bead. The relevant facts are copied here because an isolated DDx worker cannot access the Niflheim repository or `/tmp` logs.

1. Decomposed parent kept consuming attempts and escalated to a stronger model anyway.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-64217cc5`, title "Instrument cold replicator with structured per-operation S3 tracing".
- Observed result bundle: `.ddx/executions/20260606T152006-a4ca7254/result.json` plus worker logs from 2026-06-05/2026-06-06.
- Routing showed escalation exists: repeated routes started at `gpt-5.4-mini` with requested min powers 5, 7, 8, and 9, then reached `gpt-5.5` at requested min power 10.
- Metrics on the bead showed `attempt_count=24`, `total_tokens=19371353`, and average duration about `244112ms`.
- Despite triage recommending `close_decomposed_or_mark_execution_ineligible`, retries continued until the latest event recorded:
  `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`
  `powerClass=standard`
  `rationale: status: open`
  `orchestrator_action: decompose`
  `reason: parent bead exceeds the Step 0 size threshold and is already decomposed into focused child beads for tracing, counters, tests, and runbook work.`
  `child_beads: niflheim-f03c61a2, niflheim-df407aa8, niflheim-7a421ede, niflheim-0bbed6c9`
  `outcome_reason=decomposed`
- Expected DDx behavior: once the worker has reliable evidence that the bead is a decomposed parent or execution-ineligible parent, no further implementation attempt or model escalation should occur. It should mark execution-ineligible/proposed/operator-attention according to policy and move on.

2. Land/evidence coordination failures were treated like implementation failure and retried expensively.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-c75aba30`, title "Materializer per-merge memory footprint OOMs even at concurrent_limit=8".
- Two expensive attempts both used `gpt-5.4-mini`; no useful stronger-model escalation was observed.
- Metrics showed `attempt_count=2`, `total_tokens=65513510`, and average duration about `3306461.5ms`.
- First attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T113550-7b29ee42/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Implementation result commit was `7aaeb0712...`; a merge commit `2c6f4d6e6` was later observed as an ancestor of HEAD, but the result still recorded a land coordination failure.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: restoring refs/heads/main to ba2d3059f14e834dcb147f2a6db3b294d83b0fac after evidence commit failed: git update-ref refs/heads/main: fatal: update_ref failed for ref 'refs/heads/main': cannot lock ref 'refs/heads/main': is at 5a176b332b182f2a959614f63769d835c47ffd97 but expected 2c6f4d6e6961b8a93742cde66bceec056934655a`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- Second attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T142223-94f8f0f8/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Error detail:

Parent bead ddx-130cf9ab is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable.
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./... passes.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events_attachment":"ddx-3892c623/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3892c623","issue_type":"task","labels":["ac-quality:needs-refinement","area:decomposition","area:lifecycle","area:tracker","decomposed","kind:correctness","kind:safety"],"lifecycle-migrated-at":"2026-07-02T22:52:48Z","lifecycle-migrated-from-status":"closed","lifecycle-migrated-to-status":"closed","needs-human-reason":"description is truncated — part-3 implementation scope is hidden","needs-human-since":"2026-06-17T19:25:21Z","needs-human-source":"ddx work","needs-human-suggested-action":"review intake result and accept, rewrite, split, block, or cancel","needs-human-summary":"pre-claim intake blocked execution","parent":"ddx-130cf9ab","priority":0,"queue-rank":1900,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"Reject bead dep edges that re-introduce the parent or any ancestor: part: part 3","updated_at":"2026-06-17T20:06:07.697695301Z"}

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1885-1903 tells execute-bead agents that Step 0 may run `ddx bead create` for decomposition. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1981-1984 reiterates that Step 0 allows bead creation while current-bead lifecycle stays orchestrator-owned. The prompt requires `parent=<parent-id>`, but DDx does not enforce that an execute-bead-created child has the current bead as parent, inherits required labels/spec-id, or writes a valid `no_changes_rationale.txt` before mutating the real landing tracker. The dogfood attempt created top-level sample fixtures instead.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a guarded decomposition command/path for execute-bead Step 0, or validate all bead-create mutations made from an execution worktree before they reach the landing tracker.
- Reject or quarantine execute-bead-created beads whose title/description/AC look like sample fixtures, whose parent is empty or not the current bead, or whose required inherited labels/spec-id are absent.
- Add cleanup/recovery that parks or cancels invalid child beads automatically and records an operator_attention event naming the invalid IDs.
- Ensure stale run-state from a killed worker does not keep `ddx work status` reporting active work after the owning worker process is gone.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove legitimate Step 0 decomposition. Do not rewrite or delete tracker history manually. Do not change normal operator-invoked `ddx bead create` behavior outside execute-bead/work execution contexts.

Documentation in docs/ explains the agent-self-commit contract, the rationale-file fallback, and the new 'agent_silent_exit' classification.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-30T12:43:25Z","claimed-machine":"sindri","claimed-pid":"3967690","closing_commit_sha":"cedf316fc6d5082eff11bff2f38ee5618958b20f","created_at":"2026-04-30T00:10:22.985451193Z","description":"Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. The post-merge codex reviewer correctly flags BLOCK with rationale 'change set only adds execution metadata files'.

Concrete evidence (axon repo, 2026-04-29):

  Attempt: 20260429T235353-d14f6b91 (axon-80979cb8 / A1 sub-bead)
  Harness: claude / model: sonnet
  Duration: 817 sec, cost $3.61, 132 tool calls
  Tool breakdown: 79 Bash, 34 Read, 11 Grep, 8 Edit
  Edit targets: ui/src/lib/api.ts, ui/src/lib/policy-evaluator.ts,
    ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte, ui/tests/e2e/link-policy-retry.spec.ts
  Iteration commit (ddx-land-coordinator): contains exactly 2 files —
    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/result.json (23 lines)
    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/usage.json (9 lines)
  Net: zero actual edits landed despite $3.61 of model time spent making them.

The same pattern appeared on:
  20260429T235406-20eeaf2c (axon-db7a6d0a, claude/sonnet, $0.57, 4.2 min, BLOCK)
  20260429T190657-fa91f49b (axon-ab2e52e0, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.08, 24s, stalled)
  20260429T190634-579df880 (axon-80979cb8, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.12, 104s, BLOCK)
  20260429T190646-5aa5b46e (axon-db7a6d0a, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.06, 25s, BLOCK)

Total burn today: ~$4.50 across 5 attempts on 3 beads, 0 closed.

The execute-bead prompt template tells the agent to commit:
  '4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with git add <specific-paths>...'

So agent self-commit is the contract. But:

(a) When typecheck/lint/test fails, the agent is told to fix or write no_changes_rationale.txt — but apparently many agents are bailing silently without writing the rationale either, so the harness sees a clean worktree (no diff staged or committed) and treats it as 'agent made no commits'. The iteration then carries only the auto-generated execution evidence.

(b) There may also be a path where the agent commits but a pre-commit hook silently rejects it. Have not confirmed; the worktree was reaped before I could inspect.

The issue spans multiple harnesses (claude, codex via embedded openrouter), so it is not specific to one provider's commit semantics. Either:
  - The harness should reject 'agent made no commits AND no no_changes_rationale.txt' as a separate failure class instead of silently letting ddx-land-coordinator land an empty iteration
  - The agent prompt should make rationale-writing on incomplete work non-optional with stronger language
  - The harness should run a final-stage check 'did the agent produce ANY signal — commit OR rationale — and if not, treat as harness-execution-failed and surface to the operator'","id":"ddx-4e0a4a59","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:harness","area:execute-bead","kind:correctness"],"owner":"erik","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence","updated_at":"2026-04-30T12:43:46.370829758Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-30T12:43:25.451101407Z"}

4. shouldSuppressNoProgress unchanged for genuine no-progress (BaseRev == ResultRev AND not Disrupted AND no rationale).
5. New diagnostic event `disruption_detected` with kind, reason, and bead context — emitted when Disrupted is set so operators can see disruption rates.
6. (Optional) Exponential backoff for non-Disrupted no-progress: first failure 5min; second 30min; third 2h; cap at 24h. Reset on success. If implementing: replace flat noProgressCooldown with this curve; document in code comment.

   - TestLoop_DisruptionEventEmitted — assert disruption_detected event appears in bead events

Documentation: update FEAT-006 (or wherever the cooldown policy is documented) to describe Disrupted vs no-progress and the policy rationale.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:17:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"163374","closing_commit_sha":"89473d084d436e925934549d31f560e5d6b8702a","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:47:21.609526243Z","description":"The current cooldown logic treats any ExecutionFailed status as evidence the model can't make progress, applying noProgressCooldown=6h when BaseRev == ResultRev (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1074-1086 via shouldSuppressNoProgress at line 1550). This silently freezes important work whenever a worker is disrupted (killed, server restart, network blip, OS issue) — disruption produces ExecutionFailed + no commit, indistinguishable from "model genuinely tried and produced nothing."

OBSERVED (today, 2026-05-02)
After the picker fix investigation found the four "starved" P0 beads (ddx-dc157075, ddx-aee651ec, ddx-29058e2a, ddx-9d55601f) all had work-retry-after timestamps ~3 hours in the future. Investigation per ddx-9d55601f confirmed these were NOT genuine model failures — they were prior worker disruptions during this development session (worker kills, server restarts) being treated as model-gave-up.

User directive: "Cooldown should be reserved for cases where the model is truly unable to make progress on a task."

CURRENT COOLDOWN POLICY (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go)
- LandConflictCooldown = 15min — short cooldown after merge/land conflicts (line 64)
- noProgressCooldown = 6h default — when shouldSuppressNoProgress is true (line 367)
- MaxLoopCooldown = 24h — push_failed, push_conflict, decomposition decline (line 57)

shouldSuppressNoProgress (line 1550): returns true if BaseRev != "" AND ResultRev != "" AND BaseRev == ResultRev. This catches:
- Model wrote nothing AND came back cleanly (true no_progress) ✓
- Worker killed mid-execution before commit (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Network/provider error before model started (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Routing preflight rejection (FALSE no_progress — config issue)

The mis-classification is the bug.

PROPOSED FIX

Add a Disposition::Disrupted variant (or equivalent flag on ExecuteBeadReport) that explicitly marks results that are NOT evidence of model inability:
- ctx.Err() != nil (cancellation, deadline, parent died)
- syscall.SIGTERM / SIGINT received during execution
- Provider-side 5xx, connection refused, timeout (transport disruption)
- Worker process killed (parent never received clean result)
- Server-restart-detected marker
- Routing preflight rejection (operator config issue, not model failure)

For Disrupted: skip cooldown entirely (or use a tiny defer like 30s) and unclaim the bead so the next worker pickup is immediate.
For genuine no-progress (model returned clean with no commit AND rationale present): keep current 6h cooldown.

ALTERNATIVE: exponential backoff per bead. First failure: 5min; second: 30min; third: 2h; cap at 24h. Resets on any successful attempt or operator clear. This is more forgiving than the current flat 6h on the first hiccup.

PROBABLY BOTH: Disrupted skips cooldown entirely; non-Disrupted failures use exponential backoff starting at 5min, not flat 6h.

DETECTION POINTERS

- ctx.Err() check at the call site that produces ExecutionFailed (line 513, 629, 637, 1011)
- Check for `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/no_changes_rationale.txt` presence — if missing, the model never wrote one (likely disruption)
- Check the executor's exit code: SIGKILL/SIGTERM = disruption; clean exit with empty result = no-progress
- Track parent process restart count or boot ID; if changed since the attempt started, mark Disrupted

NOT IN SCOPE
- Changing the cooldown duration constants themselves
- Reworking the per-bead retry policy framework (separate concern)
- Changing land-conflict or push-failed cooldowns (those are correct as-is)
- The exponential-backoff alternative is OPTIONAL; primary fix is Disrupted classification

INTERSECTION WITH OTHER BEADS
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): the stay-alive fix prevents some classes of disruption (worker no longer exits on empty poll), but does NOT fix the cooldown-on-disruption mis-classification
- ddx-076147ee (worker client-server architecture TD): if workers become long-lived clients, server-restart disruption goes away by construction. But this fix is needed BEFORE that TD is implemented because the disruption classification is needed regardless.
- ddx-9228a484 C9 (StopCondition enum): may absorb this — the StopCondition enum could include Disrupted as a state. Verify and align.","events_attachment":"ddx-5b3e57f4/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-5b3e57f4","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","kind:fix","observed-failure"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-59cfc8c5","status":"closed","title":"work: distinguish worker-disrupted from model-gave-up; do not cooldown on disruption","updated_at":"2026-05-03T02:30:29.374776912Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-03T02:29:24.332622724Z"}

Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events_attachment":"ddx-63780ef6/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-63780ef6","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:tracker","area:lifecycle","area:decomposition","kind:safety","kind:correctness","ac-quality:needs-refinement","decomposed"],"lifecycle-migrated-at":"2026-07-02T22:52:48Z","lifecycle-migrated-from-status":"closed","lifecycle-migrated-to-status":"closed","parent":"ddx-e19185aa","priority":0,"queue-rank":1790,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-7e9e7432","status":"closed","title":"Reject bead dep edges that re-introduce the parent or any ancestor: part 1","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:34.845842756Z"}

This is the test gap the opus test-plan review identified as the most critical coverage hole: every "concurrency" or "merge race" test today uses in-memory fakes with per-worker orchestrators, so the shared-writer invariant the meta-bead design depends on is never actually exercised.

## Test infrastructure helper

Build a shared helper `newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T) (projectRoot, initialRev string)` in a new file `cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go` that:

1. Creates a temp dir via t.TempDir()
2. Runs `git init` with scrubbed GIT_* env (so the test's own pre-commit hook env doesn't leak)
3. Creates and commits an initial seed commit on `main`
4. Seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with N ready beads (parameterized by test)
5. Returns projectRoot + the initial SHA

The helper must NOT inherit any GIT_DIR from the parent test environment.

## Test cases

Create cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go with these cases. Each runs against the script harness and real git:

1. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_SingleBead_AppendLine_Merged** — one bead, directive appends a line and commits, asserts git log main shows +1 commit, worktree is cleaned up, bead closed with closing_commit_sha.

2. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_NoOp_ClassifiedAsNoChanges** — directive no-op, asserts outcome=no-changes, bead stays open (below adjudication threshold), no commit on main.

3. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_DirtyWorktreeSynthesized** — directive creates files but omits explicit commit, asserts SynthesizeCommit fires, outcome=merged, single synthetic commit on main with the expected files.

4. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FailedExit_WithCommits_Preserved** — directive commits then set-exit 1, asserts outcome=preserved, refs/ddx/iterations/<bead>/<ts>-<sha> exists, main unchanged.

5. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_LinearHistory** — seed 5 distinct beads, spawn 5 workers concurrently (single store, single projectRoot), assert `git log --merges main | wc -l` is 0, `git rev-list main ^initial | wc -l` is 5, every bead closed. **This is the ddx-6aa50e57 acceptance test that cannot be written today.**

6. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_TwoWorkersSameBead_ClaimedOnce** — seed 1 bead, spawn 2 workers against the same store, assert exactly one executes and the other reports no_ready_work. **This is the ddx-3315dce2 acceptance test.**

7. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_RebaseConflict_Preserved** — two beads both modify the same line in the same file, workers start from the same base, first lands cleanly, second gets rebase conflict and is preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/. Main has exactly +1 commit, ref exists for bead 2. (Only passes once the land coordinator from ddx-8746d8a6 is in place.)

8. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_ContextCancelBetweenIterations** — two beads, cancel context after first completes, assert second bead is not claimed. **This is the regression for commit 21d16e6.**

9. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_NoChangesRationale_ClosesBeadFast** — directive writes a no_changes_rationale.txt citing a commit SHA, asserts the bead closes as already_satisfied without the 3-strike cooldown wait.

Each test runs in isolation, cleans up its temp dir, and completes in under 10s. The full suite runs in under 60s. Use `-race` to catch any coordinator goroutine races.

## Out of scope

- Server-layer wiring (that's bead C)
- Real-AI acceptance tests (gated tier 4)
- Multi-project isolation (a separate bead, not filed here)
- Cross-machine scenarios (multi-machine topology bead)","id":"ddx-6f6bf12a","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:testing","kind:integration","phase:build"],"parent":"ddx-8746d8a6","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-43009cc","status":"closed","title":"Integration tests for work orchestration using script harness + real git","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:26:48.671814475Z"}

NON-SCOPE

- Decomposition, push_failed, or push_conflict routing.
- Any change to the canonical status vocabulary beyond the existing triage-label contract.

DDx currently increments no_changes counters and parses no_changes rationale, but the try layer does not expose a sufficiently explicit lifecycle action for the work loop to distinguish already satisfied, needs human, unjustified bad attempt, retryable transient, parent/decomposed, or external blocker cases. That allows downstream code to fall back to generic no_changes + retry-after behavior.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:229-248 handles StatusNoChanges by incrementing no_changes count and calling adjudicateNoChangesContract, then returns OutcomeReported with NoChangesOutcome. OutcomeReported does not encode whether TD-031 says to close, keep open, label, block, or avoid cooldown.
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:124-202 parses verified / needs_investigation / unjustified cases, but NoChangesOutcome only has Satisfied, Evidence, EventKind, EventBody, and Label. It lacks a TD-031 action category and cooldown eligibility signal.
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go covers the current shape but does not assert that needs_investigation and unjustified no_changes avoid the long retry-cooldown path.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the existing try package no_changes outcome model; do not introduce a new persisted status or separate disposition subsystem.
- Add a typed internal lifecycle action/cooldown eligibility value that names TD-031 actions in code-local terms, for example close already satisfied, keep open for human triage, keep open bad attempt, retry later transient, block external, or not executable.
- Update adjudicateNoChangesContract so verified no_changes returns the already-satisfied action, needs_investigation returns a human-triage/non-cooldown action, and unjustified returns a bad-attempt/non-long-cooldown action.
- Preserve existing event kind and label outputs so later store-mutation code can apply them without losing current evidence.
- Add focused tests for the action result shape.

NON-SCOPE
- Applying store mutations, labels, or cooldown changes in the live execute loop; dependent bead handles that.
- Queue derivation changes.
- Reconciliation of existing tracker rows.
- Skill prompt/vocabulary changes.

PARENT
- ddx-a9d130d0.

DEPS
- ddx-07e6d2fa: TD-031 must define the canonical lifecycle action semantics before code names them.

INTERSECTIONS
- Closed ddx-71a48703 relocated no_changes adjudication and labels; this bead extends that existing code rather than moving it again.
- Closed ddx-c8f79963 moved no_changes behavior into try.Attempt; this bead preserves that architecture.","id":"ddx-84d016dc","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","area:beads","kind:refactor","reliability","bead-lifecycle","spec:TD-031"],"owner":"codex","parent":"ddx-a9d130d0","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"TD-031","status":"closed","title":"agent: return TD-031 lifecycle action from no_changes adjudication","updated_at":"2026-05-05T21:19:17.785687902Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-05T21:17:10.570317886Z"}

2. `TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunnerAllowsConfiguredLongGate` or equivalent proves the verification timeout is configurable/worker-derived and can run a command longer than 60s without forcing `no_changes_unverified`.
3. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerifiedLongCommandCloses` proves a `verification_command` that runs longer than the old 60s limit but exits 0 closes the bead as `already_satisfied` and emits `no_changes_verified`.
4. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerificationTimeoutKeepsOpenAndReaps` proves timeout keeps the bead open with `triage:no-changes-unverified`, records timeout evidence, and leaves no verification process tree behind.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'Test(DefaultVerificationCommandRunner|ExecuteBeadWorkerNoChanges.*Verification)' -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","closing_commit_sha":"f609f22ef84a0d7b05ba89dd3e9fd5b92fbfa446","created_at":"2026-05-15T13:09:07.942514913Z","description":"PROBLEM
A `no_changes_rationale.txt` with `verification_command:` can time out, leave its child process tree running, and let `ddx work --watch` move on to another bead while the old verification is still consuming the project. Live reproduction on 2026-05-15 in `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau`: `fizeau-bc9586d7` returned `verification_command: sh -lc "go test ... && lefthook run pre-commit"`; the watcher logged `• fizeau-bc9586d7 → no_changes` and started `fizeau-7af3f548` at 13:07:21Z, while `ps` still showed orphaned PID 3510889 (`sh -lc go test ...`) and child PID 3512539 (`go test ./... -count=1`) under PPID 1.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:117-128` hard-codes `DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout = 60 * time.Second` and runs the command with `exec.CommandContext(cctx, "/bin/sh", "-c", command)`. `exec.CommandContext` kills only the direct shell on timeout; because workers commonly emit nested shell commands such as `sh -lc "go test ..."`, the actual test process survives as an orphan. The 60s timeout is also shorter than normal bead acceptance gates like `go test ./...` plus `lefthook run pre-commit`, so legitimate already-satisfied checks are classified as unverified/no-progress instead of closing the bead.

PROPOSED FIX
Run no_changes verification commands with the same process-tree discipline used by agent executors: start the shell in its own process group on Unix, terminate the group on timeout/cancel, and wait/reap before returning. Replace the hard-coded 60s ceiling with a worker/config-derived timeout that is long enough for normal acceptance gates, or make it configurable with a conservative default. Preserve the contract: exit 0 closes as `already_satisfied`; non-zero/timeout keeps the bead open with clear `no_changes_unverified` evidence.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken no_changes verification semantics. Do not let verification run asynchronously. Do not close a bead without a passing command. Do not change readiness classification or route selection.","events_attachment":"ddx-8a0aea8e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-8a0aea8e","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:work","kind:bug","operator-ux","downstream:fizeau","dirty-tree"],"priority":0,"queue-rank":600,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-af6f8fc7","status":"closed","title":"work: reap and correctly budget no_changes verification commands","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:29.795710244Z"}

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable.
...[truncated]

- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable. Test: ddx_doctor_detects_and_repairs_back_edge.
- ddx work no-progress remediation: when an attempted bead's outcome is 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale' AND any freshly-created child carries the attempted bead in its deps, ddx auto-emits an 'operator_attention' event with reason 'parent_back_edge' and stops re-claiming that bead. (Today it loops indefinitely.) Test: ddx_work_halts_on_parent_back_edge.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads.
